commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer label, security/auth-provider API surface, an insufficient real-behavior proof path, and a related open contract proposal make this a human contract-review item rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the diff; it adds no workflow or dependency source change and reuses existing exec SecretRef guardrails, while still needing mai... (sha=f3b376314726c84879985712cc060d860ae6036e)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `secretProviderIntegrations` manifest metadata, a SecretRef exec-provider preset materializer, an SDK type subpath, docs, tests, export metadata, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR adds a public plugin manifest and SDK contract. Source inspection gives high-confidence current-main comparison because the proposed manifest field is absent while the existing exec SecretRef runtime is present.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label, security/auth-provider API surface, an insufficient real-behavior proof path, and a related open contract proposal make this a human contract-review item rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: Add plugin manifest contract for SecretRef provider integrations
target.body:
## Summary
- add a manifest-level SecretRef provider integration contract for plugins
- let plugin manifests declare exec provider presets that materialize existing `secrets.providers` config
- expose SDK types and cover manifest normalization/materialization with focused tests

## Why
External providers like Vault, OneCLI, 1Password, Bitwarden, and similar stores were already possible through manual exec SecretRef configuration. This makes them discoverable, portable, and installable as plugins without hardcoding provider-specific logic in OpenClaw core.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: External SecretRef providers could already be wired manually with `exec`, but plugin-owned Vault-style integrations were not discoverable or portable through plugin manifest metadata.
- Real environment tested: Local Podman setup with `quay.io/sallyom/openclaw:secrets`, `hashicorp/vault:latest` in dev mode, external `claw-vault` plugin from https://github.com/sallyom/claw-vault, and OpenRouter model `openrouter/openai/gpt-4.1-nano`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started Vault dev mode, seeded `secret/providers/openrouter` from `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, started OpenClaw with `models.providers.openrouter.apiKey` set to `{ "source": "exec", "provider": "vault", "id": "providers/openrouter/apiKey" }`, ran `node dist/index.js secrets reload --json --timeout 30000`, then ran `node dist/index.js agent --agent main --message "Reply with exactly: vault-secretref-ok" --timeout 60 --json`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Full terminal-command repro is in this Gist: https://gist.github.com/sallyom/50f4678b053eaa93c4311a5509e52aeb. Copied live output from `secrets reload`: `{"ok":true,"warningCount":0}`. Copied live output from the agent run included `vault-secretref-ok`.
- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw reloaded SecretRefs with zero warnings and completed a live OpenRouter agent turn using the API key resolved from Vault by the external `claw-vault` exec resolver.
- What was not tested: Production Vault deployment, non-dev Vault auth methods, 1Password/Bitwarden/OneCLI plugins, and a finished OpenClaw CLI/UI command that materializes discovered presets.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this contract, the same Vault resolver could only be wired by hand through explicit `secrets.providers.vault` config; there was no plugin manifest preset for OpenClaw to discover/materialize.

## Verification
- `pnpm vitest run src/secrets/provider-integrations.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
target.author: sallyom

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
